ddls:
CREATE TABLE "movies" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movie */,
  "movie_name" TEXT /* Title of the movie */,
  "director_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the director of the movie */,
  "genres" TEXT /* Genres of the movie (e.g., Action, Drama, Comedy) */,
  "imdb_score" REAL /* IMDb score of the movie */,
  "facebook_likes" INTEGER /* Number of Facebook likes for the movie */,
  "critic_reviews" INTEGER /* Number of critic reviews for the movie */,
  "user_votes" INTEGER /* Number of user votes for the movie */,
  "user_reviews" INTEGER /* Number of user reviews for the movie */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of release of the movie */,
  "production_company" TEXT /* Production company of the movie */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget of the movie */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Revenue of the movie */,
  "marketing_campaign_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the marketing campaign associated with the movie */,
  "twitter_handle" TEXT /* Twitter handle of the movie */,
  "instagram_handle" TEXT /* Instagram handle of the movie */,
  "runtime" INTEGER /* Runtime of the movie in minutes */,
  "rating" TEXT /* MPAA rating of the movie */,
  "country_of_production" TEXT /* Country where the movie was produced */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movies_director_id FOREIGN KEY ("director_id") REFERENCES directors ("director_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movies_marketing_campaign_id FOREIGN KEY ("marketing_campaign_id") REFERENCES marketing_campaigns ("campaign_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "directors" (
  "director_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each director */,
  "director_name" TEXT /* Full name of the director */,
  "birth_year" INTEGER /* Year of birth of the director */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the director */,
  "biography" TEXT /* Biographical information about the director */,
  "awards" TEXT /* Awards won by the director */,
  "notable_works" TEXT /* Notable works of the director */,
  "years_of_experience" INTEGER /* Years of experience in the film industry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("director_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_reviews" (
  "review_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each review */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie being reviewed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who wrote the review */,
  "review_text" TEXT /* Text of the review */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given by the user (1-10) */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date when the review was written */,
  "helpfulness_votes" INTEGER /* Number of helpfulness votes for the review */,
  "spoilers" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the review contains spoilers */,
  "sentiment_analysis" TEXT /* Sentiment analysis score (positive, negative, neutral) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("review_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_reviews_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_reviews_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., user, admin, moderator) */,
  "registration_date" TEXT /* Date when the user registered on the platform */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the user */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the user */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user */,
  "activity_level" INTEGER /* Activity level of the user (e.g., number of reviews, posts, etc.) */,
  "preferences" TEXT /* User preferences (e.g., favorite genres, actors, etc.) */,
  "verification_status" INTEGER /* Verification status of the user account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "marketing_campaigns" (
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each marketing campaign */,
  "campaign_name" TEXT /* Name of the marketing campaign */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the campaign */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the campaign */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget allocated for the campaign */,
  "platforms" TEXT /* Platforms used for the campaign (e.g., Facebook, Twitter, Google Ads) */,
  "clicks" INTEGER /* Number of clicks generated by the campaign */,
  "impressions" INTEGER /* Number of impressions generated by the campaign */,
  "conversions" INTEGER /* Number of conversions generated by the campaign */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("campaign_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audience_demographics" (
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic entry */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "age_range" TEXT /* Age range of the audience (e.g., 18-24, 25-34) */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the audience */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the audience */,
  "percentage" REAL /* Percentage of the audience in this demographic */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audience_demographics_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_social_media" (
  "social_media_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each social media entry */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "platform" TEXT /* Social media platform (e.g., Facebook, Twitter, Instagram) */,
  "followers" INTEGER /* Number of followers on the platform */,
  "engagement_rate" REAL /* Engagement rate on the platform */,
  "total_posts" INTEGER /* Total number of posts on the platform */,
  "average_likes_per_post" INTEGER /* Average number of likes per post */,
  "average_comments_per_post" INTEGER /* Average number of comments per post */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("social_media_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_social_media_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)
